source.section: Part 15 - Navigation & Case III Recovery > 6 - Markpoints > 6.3 - How to Use Markpoints

steps.action: We will assume Markpoint #2 (M2) has already been created beforehand with the Target Designate method mentioned earlier.
steps.voiced: Confirm markpoint has already been created using Target Designate.

steps.action: Select FLIR page and press the Sensor Control Switch in the direction of the selected DDI.
steps.voiced: Select the FLIR page and press the Sensor Control Switch toward the selected DDI.

steps.action: The Select Focus Diamond indicates what display is selected.
steps.voiced: Confirm Select Focus Diamond shows the correct DDI is selected.

steps.action: On HSI (Horizontal Situation Indicator) page, select desired markpoint using the OSBs (Option Select Button) next to UP or DOWN arrows.
steps.voiced: On the HSI page, use the UP or DOWN OSBs to select the desired markpoint.

steps.action: Press OSB next to WYPT (boxed = selected)
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to WYPT — confirm it boxes.

steps.action: Press on OSB next to WPSDG to designate waypoint/markpoint as the target point (boxed = selected)
steps.voiced: Press the OSB next to WPSDG to designate the markpoint as target — confirm it boxes.

steps.action: Targeting pod will automatically slew to the selected waypoint/markpoint. 6 - MARKPOINTS
steps.voiced: Confirm targeting pod auto-slews to the selected markpoint.

steps.action: Use the TDC (Throttle Designator Controller) Aft/Fwd/Left/Right controls to slew the targeting pod reticle over the desired target if adjustments are required.
steps.voiced: Slew the TDC to fine-adjust the targeting pod reticle over the target.

steps.action: You can then use a weapon of your choice like a Maverick and fire it on the designated point (markpoint in WPDSG).
steps.voiced: Weapon is now slaved to the designated markpoint — clear to employ.
